Factbox-Before Intel, which CEOs drew Trump's ire? While Trump's demand marked a rare presidential attempt to determine who leads a corporation, Tan is not the first executive or company to face Trump's ire - the president has been vocal about his complaints concerning corporate policies and operations since he took office in January.  Trump's demand for the Intel CEO's departure came after Reuters reported exclusively in April that Tan invested at least $200 million in hundreds of Chinese advanced manufacturing and chip firms, some of which were linked to the Chinese military.
